chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI CP D 1493 of 2025 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on CMA No.7507/2025. 2. For hearing of main case. 24.04.2025 Mr. Iftikhar Hussain, advocate for the petitioner. On the last date, following order was passed: “17.04.2025 Mr. Iftikhar Hussain, advocate for the petitioner. 1. Urgency granted. 2. Exemption granted subject to all just exceptions. 3&4. Counsel is confronted as the issue appear to have been determined vide order apparent at page 151 of the petition. To come up on 24.04.2025.” Briefly stated that CP D 5367 of 2024 and CP D 5368 of 2024 filed by the petitioner were disposed of vide following order: “27.01.2025 Mr. Muzzamil Hussain Advocate for the Petitioners. Mr. Agha Shahid Majeed and Mr. Khalid Mehmood Rajpar, Advocates for the Respondent (F.B.R) Ms. Alizeh Bashir, Assistant Attorney General. Mr. Shiraz Ahmed, Director PCA and Mr. Imran Saifi, A.O. PCA are present. Parawise comments filed on behalf of respondent Nos.1 and 2 are taken on record. Learned counsel for the Respondent (F.B.R) states that the assessment is held-up due to non-production of documents/information on the part of the information/documentation sought shall be provided, whereafter the Department may pass a final order. It is jointly stated that these petitions may be disposed of as aforesaid. Order accordingly. Office is directed to place copy of this order in connected petition.” In view thereof, no case is made out to entertain the subsequent petition on the same cause, therefore, petition is found to be mis- conceived, hence, dismissed in limine.

Order accordingly. Office is directed to place copy of this order in connected petition.” In view thereof, no case is made out to entertain the subsequent petition on the same cause, therefore, petition is found to be mis- conceived, hence, dismissed in limine. Judge Judge
